title: Refactor auth system to use JWT with AuthService
description: Consolidate dual auth paths by replacing UserStore/SessionStore-based authentication with JWT+Payload AuthService. Update auth-middleware.ts to use AuthService instead of UserStore/SessionStore, refactor API routes in src/api/auth/ to consistently use AuthService, and remove deprecated in-memory stores. The Payload user schema already has correct RBAC roles (admin/editor/viewer).
existing_patterns: src/auth/auth-service.ts - Payload-based auth with JWT tokens, password verification using PBKDF2 matching Payload's algorithm
src/auth/jwt-service.ts - Custom HMAC-SHA256 JWT with access/refresh token rotation and generation-based session invalidation
src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts - Express-style middleware factory pattern with rate limiting
src/middleware/role-guard.ts - Role-based access middleware using ROLE_HIERARCHY
src/collections/Users.ts - Payload collection with role field using admin/editor/viewer values already aligned with RbacRole
questions: Should UserStore and SessionStore be removed entirely or kept as deprecated/backup?
Are there any API routes in src/app/api/auth/ that need to be updated to ensure they all use the same auth path?
Should the migration include updating the Payload user collection schema for the new RbacRole, or is that already aligned?
